domain: Queen of Svarga — wife of Indra — commander of divine armies
primary_quality: Nurture through strength — commanding loyalty through love — divine authority that protects rather than dominates
story_use: For heroes who lead through love not fear — who command loyalty by being willing to die for their people first
notes: Airavata the white elephant makes Indrani the most direct deity reference for elephant-bonded heroes

domain: Sacred river — purification — descent from heaven
primary_quality: The river that cannot be owned — flows where it will — cannot be dammed forever — cleanses all it touches
weapon_or_symbol: Her body is the river — the current is her weapon
story_use: For river-bonded heroes — for stories of purification and unstoppable flow
notes: Ganga also connects to the Mahabharata through Bheeshma — his mother — the river who produced the unbreakable vow

domain: God of cosmic waters — ocean — moral law
primary_quality: The ocean that does not apologise — the tide that does not explain itself — the sovereign of the deep who sees all oaths
story_use: For coastal and water-domain heroes — for stories where the truth of the sea (that it is indifferent) is the lesson
notes: Varuna is one of the oldest Vedic deities — predates the Puranic pantheon — his moral authority is pre-dharmic in some sense

domain: Supreme goddess — destroyer of what dharma cannot tolerate — the power of the divine feminine made weapon
primary_quality: The fierce force that does not wait for permission — the mother who becomes terrifying to protect
weapon_or_symbol: Trishula + sword + lotus + conch + more (multiple arms)
story_use: For female heroes of fierce protection — for the moment when protection stops being gentle and becomes absolute
notes: Durga's connection to Mahishasumardini (slayer of the buffalo demon) is direct for Chhattisgarh — the buffalo demon and the buffalo hero are mirror images

domain: Goddess of time transformation and liberation through destruction
primary_quality: The liberation that comes through complete transformation — the dancing on the corpse — death as the ultimate teacher
relevant_heroes: All hero stories at their darkest moment
story_use: Kali appears in Hulmane-The-Saga as the force that a character must pass through to emerge changed — she is the furnace not the destination
notes: Kali is also the primary dark-deity reference for many villains who invoke her consuming aspect rather than her liberating aspect

domain: The preserver — the one who sustains the cosmos between creation and dissolution
primary_quality: The twelve forms that maintain the universe — the deity who takes avatars specifically to restore dharma when it fails
weapon_or_symbol: Sudarshana Chakra + Kaumodaki mace + Shankha conch + Padma lotus
story_use: For heroes whose role is to maintain balance — who hold the centre — whose power is in preservation not creation or destruction
notes: The Dashavatara (10 avatars) of Vishnu is the most referenced divine structure in Hulmane-The-Saga — see vishnu-avatars.csv

domain: The destroyer and regenerator — the cosmic ascetic — the dancer — the mountain — the teacher of ultimate wisdom
primary_quality: The destruction that enables new creation — the silence that contains all sound — the ascetic who is also the lover
story_use: For mountain heroes — for guru characters — for the moment of necessary destruction before growth — for the dance that holds the cosmos
notes: Shiva has more forms than any other deity — see shiva-forms.csv for the full list of which aspects apply to which heroes

domain: Remover of obstacles — lord of groups — patron of new beginnings — the scribe who wrote the Mahabharata
primary_quality: The wisdom of the right beginning — the recognition that every obstacle is also a test — the intelligence of the elephant
weapon_or_symbol: Modaka + broken tusk + Ankush goad + Pasha noose
relevant_heroes: All heroes at the beginning of their journey
story_use: Ganesha is invoked at every beginning in Hulmane-The-Saga — the tattoo system starts with a pawprint/footprint — that first mark is Ganesha's blessing
notes: See ganesha-attributes.csv for which specific Ganesha aspect applies to which hero

domain: God of war and the divine warrior — patron of Tamil arts and mountain peoples — the eternal youth who never ages
primary_quality: Perfect precision in battle — the general who never fails — youth as a form of perfection not inexperience
weapon_or_symbol: Vel (divine spear) + Peacock vehicle as battlefield
story_use: For warrior heroes whose precision is absolute — for Tamil heroes specifically — for the chakram as a Kartikeya weapon form
notes: In South India (especially Tamil Nadu) Kartikeya/Murugan is the primary deity — his connection to mountains and the Kurinji flower makes him essential for Tamil Nadu

domain: The sage-warrior avatar — the one who cleared corrupt power from the earth 21 times — the axe as both weapon and tool
primary_quality: Righteous anger that has been patient for a very long time — and when it strikes it strikes once and completely
animal_vehicle: None — he is already the avatar
relevant_heroes: kerala (Parashurama hero directly named) + maharashtra (Shivagati indirect)
story_use: For heroes who carry the weight of inherited duty — for the axe as a weapon of clearing rather than conquest
notes: The Parashurama avatar is unique: he is the only avatar who does not retire — he still exists — still carries his axe — still training warriors. Kerala's hero is his continuation.

domain: The divine charioteer — the cosmic teacher — the one who reveals the infinite in the ordinary — love as cosmic force
primary_quality: The wisdom that enables others to act — the truth told in paradox — beauty as the highest form of instruction
relevant_heroes: manipur (Moirangthem — directly — Krishna visited Manipur) + uttar-pradesh (Gangaputra)
story_use: For the wisest heroes — for the Bhagavad Gita moment — for the character who guides without fighting — for the dance that is also truth
notes: Krishna's visit to Manipur and marriage to Chitrangada is a direct Mahabharata connection — Manipur's hero carries this lineage

domain: The sun god — source of all energy — father of Karna in the Mahabharata — the light that comes before all things
primary_quality: The power that comes first — first light — the advantage of being earliest — but also the isolation of being first
animal_vehicle: Crow (as vehicle of Shani his son) — Garuda (indirect)
story_use: For dawn-power heroes — for the Karna archetype (sun's son) — for heroes who are first and pay the price of being first
notes: Surya as Karna's divine father is the most important Mahabharata connection for Arunachal Pradesh — the dawn state's hero carries the sun's gift and the sun's tragic child's shadow

domain: The great goddess of Assam — the power in the earth itself — the yoni pitha — the most fundamental feminine force
primary_quality: Invincibility born from the earth — the armor that is not separate from the body but the body itself made sacred
weapon_or_symbol: The earth itself is her weapon — the creative power that cannot be separated from the destructive
animal_vehicle: None fixed — she IS the ground
story_use: For heroes whose power comes from the land itself — for the rhino bond specifically — for the earth as armor
notes: Kamakhya is one of the 51 Shakti Pithas — the most sacred — her power is specifically connected to the earth and to Assam as a landscape

domain: Goddess of knowledge learning arts and wisdom — the sacred river that runs underground
primary_quality: The knowledge that flows underground even when the surface is dry — the wisdom that enables without forcing
story_use: For scholars and knowledge-keepers — for heroes whose power is in what they know not what they do — for the hidden river of wisdom
notes: Saraswati as an underground river (the mythical Saraswati River) is perfectly aligned with the buried knowledge theme of multiple Hulmane-The-Saga stories

domain: King of the gods — god of thunder and lightning — wielder of the Vajra — the divine warrior-king
primary_quality: The thunderbolt that strikes once and decisively — the power that changes a battlefield in a single moment — but must be aimed by wisdom not rage
relevant_heroes: bihar (Vajramukha — named after Vajra) + karnataka (Indrani aspect for Tungabhadra)
story_use: For thunder-power heroes — for heroes who carry the weight of the divine hammer — for the moment when pure force is the right answer
notes: Indra's complexity (a flawed divine king who both serves dharma and violates it) makes him the most honest deity reference — his power and his failures both appear in the heroes he inspires

domain: The divine monkey — the devotee whose love gives him the power to cross the uncrossable
primary_quality: The power of devotion that becomes physical ability — love for the cause that makes any height possible — the sky-leaper
weapon_or_symbol: Gada mace + his own fists + Lanka's fire
animal_vehicle: None fixed — he IS the aerial force
story_use: sky-leaper quality)
notes: For heroes whose power is their devotion to what they protect — for the vertical fighter — for the hero who arrives when others say arrival is impossible
column11: Hanuman's role as the one who crosses the ocean of impossibility is essential for Shivagati — she crosses the ocean of the cliff face instead

domain: The one who chooses to remain near the world to reduce its suffering — infinite compassion as infinite power
primary_quality: Compassion as strength — the choice to protect through never harming — the warrior who wins without fighting
story_use: For the most advanced heroes — those who have reached the point where wisdom is protection — for the non-violent warrior — for the Buddhist-adjacent kingdoms
notes: Avalokitesvara as the primary deity reference for Sikkim reflects the Tibetan Buddhist tradition that is dominant there — the monastery hero needs a Buddhist divine reference not a Hindu one

domain: Lord of the Universe — the deity of Puri — the deity of all peoples (no caste in Jagannath's temple)
primary_quality: The cosmic wheel that turns regardless — the lord who belongs to all — the democracy of the divine
animal_vehicle: None fixed — the chariot is his vehicle
story_use: For heroes whose power is in inclusivity and motion — for the wheel that keeps turning — for the chariot builders who maintain what holds the cosmos
notes: Jagannath's syncretic nature (accepted by all communities) makes him ideal for Odisha's hero whose power is in building community across divides

domain: The beautiful goddess of the three cities — the supreme Shakti — the most complete divine feminine form
primary_quality: The beauty that is also the cosmos — the three cities as three levels of reality — the triple goddess of creation maintenance and dissolution
weapon_or_symbol: Sri Chakra (geometric form) + all weapons indirectly
relevant_heroes: tripura (Tripurari — paired with Tripurari Shiva form)
story_use: For the triple-fortress kingdom specifically — the goddess of the three cities for the hero named for the destroyer of the three cities
notes: The pairing of Tripurasundari (goddess of three cities) with Tripurari (Shiva who destroys them) for the Tripura hero and villain is the deepest theological mirror in Hulmane-The-Saga

domain: The mountain goddess — daughter of Himavat — consort of Shiva — the divine feminine in its most patient and most powerful form
primary_quality: The mountain that becomes the goddess — beauty that is also endurance — love that is also absolute strength
weapon_or_symbol: Trishula (shared with Shiva when she is Uma) + lotus
story_use: For mountain heroes — for the patience that is also the deepest power — for the hero who was told the mountain was too steep and climbed it anyway
notes: Parvati as mother of both Ganesha and Kartikeya connects her to the two patron deities of Hulmane-The-Saga's opening and combat themes
